kv.memory.by-design-asymmetries-do-not-f: BY-DESIGN asymmetries (do not file/fix as parity gaps): (1) rust/lichen-meshtastic does NOT consume test/vectors/meshtastic_app_compat.json because it is a prost/protobuf IP_TUNNEL_APP bridge, not a phone-API emulator - the vectors describe the Zephyr phone-API surface (lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic). (2) MeshCore compat is Zephyr-only by design (9c4y bead series); Python participates only as vector/schema validator. (3) l2_payload/announce_coords vector consumption is Python-direct + C-mirrored; Rust/C encoders for announce Type=0x01 coords don't exist (decode-only) - acceptable until they originate announces.
kv.memory.dual-impl-discipline: Rust and Zephyr are co-equal implementations BY DESIGN. Two implementations prevent 'the code is the spec' syndrome. When they disagree, the SPEC is the arbiter. Shared test vectors (gbuw) enforce this.
kv.memory.gnss-epoching-idea-from-2026-0: GNSS epoching idea from 2026-07-02 session: build-time stamps in firmware or board metadata may provide an initial epoch/bounds for GNSS time before lock; evaluate for GNSS/time sync design without requiring hardware validation in the software-only phase.
kv.memory.renode-nrf52840-validation-202: Renode nRF52840 LICHEN validation (bead r7h4.5, 2026-07-07): Renode 1.16.1 arm64 portable-dotnet installed at /mnt/lichen-zephyr/renode-arm64 (wrapper /usr/local/bin renode; needs DOTNET_SYSTEM_GLOBALIZATION_INVARIANT=1, libicu absent). Build T-Echo Renode firmware: SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=$(git log -1 --format=%ct) west build -b t_echo/nrf52840 lichen/apps/puck -d build/t_echo_renode -p always -- -DBOARD_ROOT=$PWD/lichen -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen -DEXTRA_DTC_OVERLAY_FILE=.../renode_console.overlay -DEXTRA_CONF_FILE=.../renode_console.conf. lichen-sim needs python>=3.11: use 'cd python && uv sync' then 'uv run'. Key Renode nRF gotchas: (1) firmware SX1262 is on spi1 0x40004000 which Renode models as twi1(I2C) - replace with SPI.NRF52840_SPI; (2) MUST set easyDMA:true on the SPI controller or nordic,nrf-spim times out; (3) FICR DEVICEID reads 0 -> L2 'hardware ID is all zeros' - Tag 0x10000060/0x10000064; (4) SPIM easyDMA never calls FinishTransmission - SX1262.cs resets opcode state on CS(cs-gpios P0.24) deassert via OnGPIO input 1; (5) 'sysbus Tag <range> name value' must OMIT trailing 'true' (true halts CPU on access, breaks boot). Status: boot/SPI/L2/TX PASS; RX into firmware NOT working (SX1262.cs PollRx one-shot at SetRx - bead r7h4.6; sim time-model - r7h4.7); RAK4631 platform wrong wiring - r7h4.9; CI - r7h4.8.
kv.memory.schc-libschc-evaluation-2026-0: SCHC libSCHC evaluation 2026-06-30: evaluated upstream imec-idlab/libschc at a01d7856243d3aa1242918e31649d8a5ee92872e. License is GPL-3.0 dual/commercial, compatible with LICHEN GPL C firmware. Upstream README says implementation is experimental and missing RFC 8724 features. It is plain C, not a Zephyr module, with static schc_config.h/rules/rule_config.h integration and example rule tables. Rough arm-zephyr-eabi -Os object size for example config: bit_operations 1399 text, compressor 10745 text + 268 bss, fragmenter 20015 text + 3527 bss, picocoap 3160 text, jsmn 1224 text, schc 5339 text + 40 data, rcs 4 text; total about 41.9 KiB text and 3.8 KiB BSS before Zephyr/linker GC. Current LICHEN schc.c rough object size with same compiler is 6240 text, 0 BSS. Existing LICHEN SCHC vectors pass 12/12. Recommendation: do not import/swap libSCHC for embedded nodes now; keep LICHEN profile implementation and use libSCHC only as a reference or possible future gateway/prototype dependency after a dedicated rule-table adapter and fragmentation fit study.
kv.memory.triple-impl: THREE implementations: Rust, Zephyr (C), Python. Python also runs the simulator, making it the test oracle. Three impls = even stronger spec discipline. When any two disagree, the spec decides.
kv.memory.use-firmware-or-board-build-ti: Use firmware or board build-time epoch stamps as a no-hardware GNSS/time sanity floor where a runtime GNSS epoch is otherwise unavailable; preserve explicit diagnostics for below-build-epoch samples instead of silently trusting them.
kv.memory.workflow-rule-every-small-code: Workflow rule: every code creation or modification Bead, including small fixes, must be followed by three independent codereview subagent passes before it is considered done. Record review findings in Beads notes or child/follow-up Beads, and explicitly include this in acceptance criteria for new codegen/fix Beads.
kv.memory.workspace-topology-2026-07-23-: Workspace topology 2026-07-23: /mnt/lichen-zephyr/workspace HEAD (c4e190f879) is 22025 commits BEHIND origin/main (38 ahead, merge-base 936600c332). Cross-impl parity review epic project-LICHEN-mmgu was filed against the checked-out tree. Known origin/main deltas: SCHC rule-5 link-local OSCORE bug already fixed there (schc.c:801,976); C SCHC fragment engine rewritten there to RFC-8724-style 15-bit bit-packed header (a THIRD format, still != Python/Rust); rust/lichen-schc/tests/shared_vectors.rs exists there expecting format_version==2. Reconcile/merge before implementing mmgu.3 and mmgu.5.
kv.memory.zephyr-builds-ec2: Zephyr Linux-only builds/tests should use AWS EC2 when they cannot run on local macOS. Local AWS profile: AdministratorAccess-921772462201. Persistent single-AZ EBS builder cache: vol-017cfe48bd75340d0 in us-east-2a (200GB gp3), mount at /mnt/lichen-zephyr. Contents: Zephyr SDK 0.16.8 (ARM64), west workspace (Zephyr v3.7.0), Python venv (west/pytest/ruff/mypy/cbor2/cryptography), Rust 1.96.1 (thumbv7em-none-eabihf, thumbv8m.main-none-eabihf), uv, ccache (10GB), jq, Node.js 20, Claude Code CLI 2.1.x, gh CLI. Auth tokens in /mnt/lichen-zephyr/secrets.sh (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GH_TOKEN). Instance type: c7g.xlarge or c7g.4xlarge (ARM Graviton). Usage: attach volume, run 'sudo /mnt/lichen-zephyr/scripts/mount-volume.sh', then 'source /mnt/lichen-zephyr/env.sh'. Claude can run autonomously with 'claude' command after sourcing env.sh. Keep instances short-lived and detach EBS cleanly when done.
